9	# Feature: DDx Installation Architecture
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-015
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P0
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	> **Update 2026-04-29 (project-local install, no symlinks):** This feature
17	> is amended to drop the global skill installation model entirely. DDx no
18	> longer installs skills under `~/.agents/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/`,
19	> and `ddx install --global` is removed. `ddx init` and `ddx install
20	> <plugin>` only touch the current `<projectRoot>`. The new project-local
21	> tree (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`,
22	> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`) is committed to git as real files.
23	> **Zero symlinks are created by ddx install** вЂ” see the new
24	> "Cross-platform invariant" section below for the Windows-compatibility
25	> reasoning and how plugin-author symlinks are handled. Existing projects
26	> with home-directory symlinks or stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries
27	> are handled per the new "Migration" section. Plugin manifests whose
28	> `Root.Target` begins with `~` are rejected with a non-zero exit.
29	> Sections, bullets, and acceptance criteria below that describe
30	> `ddx install --global`, `~/.ddx/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
31	> or `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` should be read as historical context;
32	> the amendment in this header and the two new sections are
33	> authoritative.
34	>
35	> **Update 2026-04-17:** The skill roster referenced throughout this
36	> document (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review,
37	> ddx-run, ddx-doctor) reflects the pre-consolidation layout. Per
38	> FEAT-011, DDx now ships a **single portable `ddx` skill** with
39	> `SKILL.md` + `reference/*.md` (progressive disclosure, agentskills.io
40	> standard). The installation flow described below is still accurate
41	> in structure вЂ” binary separate from library, `ddx init` copies skills
42	> as real files, `ddx install <plugin>` adds plugin-scoped content вЂ”
43	> but any reference to the 7-skill roster should be read as historical
44	> context. Sections that specifically describe the bootstrap
45	> allowlist are now ["ddx"] instead of ["ddx-doctor", "ddx-run"], and
46	> the stale-skill cleanup removes all old ddx-prefixed dirs (ddx-bead,
47	> ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install,
48	> ddx-release) on init and update. See FEAT-011 for the current skill
49	> architecture.
50	
51	## Overview
52	
53	Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
54	- **install.sh** вЂ” binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
55	- **ddx init** вЂ” project-local: writes `<projectRoot>/.ddx/`,
56	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
57	  as real files (copied, never symlinked). Touches no path outside the
58	  project root.
59	- **ddx install \<plugin\>** вЂ” project-local: plugin tarball extracted
60	  into `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, then plugin skills copied
61	  as real files into `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
62	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Touches no path outside the project
63	  root. Creates zero symlinks (see "Cross-platform invariant").
64	- All project-local install outputs (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`,
65	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`)
66	  are intended to be committed to git so teammates and CI get the
67	  same skills on clone without re-running `ddx install`.
68	
69	## Cross-platform invariant
70	
71	**`ddx install` (and `ddx init`) creates zero symlinks.** Every file
72	written is a real file copied from the binary's embedded tree or from
73	an extracted plugin tarball.
74	
75	**Reasoning вЂ” Windows compatibility.** Symlinks on Windows require
76	either Developer Mode, an elevated process, or specific filesystem
77	features, and behave inconsistently across NTFS, ReFS, and shared
78	filesystems. A single project-local layout that works identically on
79	Linux, macOS, and Windows is worth the duplication cost of copied
80	files.
81	
82	**Plugin-source symlinks are not materialized.** If a plugin tarball
83	contains symlinks (e.g. an author symlinks `.claude/skills/foo` в†’
84	`../.agents/skills/foo` inside their source tree), DDx does **not**
85	materialize those symlinks on extract. Plugin authors are responsible
86	for shipping each skill as a real directory in every target the
87	plugin declares. A plugin that relies on tarball symlinks resolving
88	on the install side is a malformed plugin.
89	
90	**Manifest validation.** Plugin manifests are scanned at install
91	time. Any `Root.Target` (or equivalent install target) whose path
92	begins with `~` is rejected and `ddx install` exits with a non-zero
93	status. Targets must be project-relative paths under
94	`<projectRoot>/`. This invariant is enforced even for plugins that
95	worked under the pre-amendment global model.
96	
97	## Migration
98	
99	Pre-amendment installs may have left state in three places. This
100	section describes how DDx heals or ignores each.
101	
102	- **Home-directory symlinked skills** (`~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
103	  `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` pointing into `~/.ddx/skills/` or a project
104	  tree). `ddx update --force` (and `ddx init --force` inside an
105	  existing project) heals the project by rewriting
106	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
107	  as real files. DDx does **not** delete files outside the project
108	  root; users may `rm -rf ~/.agents/skills/ddx-*
109	  ~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` themselves if they want a clean home tree.
110	
111	- **Stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries** from a prior global
112	  install. These are treated as **no-op uninstalls**: `ddx
113	  uninstall <name>` succeeds without touching the filesystem when
114	  the recorded paths are outside the current project root, and the
115	  entry is removed from `installed.yaml`. No error is raised for
116	  missing files.
117	
118	- **`~/.ddx/plugins/`** from a prior global install. DDx leaves this
119	  directory in place as inert state вЂ” nothing reads from it under
120	  the project-local model. Users may `rm -rf ~/.ddx/plugins/` at
121	  their convenience; DDx will not recreate it.
122	
123	## Problem Statement
124	
125	Current behavior:
126	- `install.sh` does too much (creates `~/.ddx/`, sets up symlinks)
127	- `ddx install helix` clones to user home (`~/.ddx/plugins/`), not project-scoped
128	- Symlinks aren't tracked by git, so project-local `.agents/skills в†’ .ddx/skills` breaks on clone
129	- No separation between global installation and project-scoped plugin management
130	
131	Desired behavior:
132	- `install.sh` does one thing: get the binary into PATH
133	- `ddx install --global` owns global skill setup (home directory)
134	- `ddx init` copies bootstrap skills as real files (git-trackable)
135	- `ddx install <plugin>` is project-scoped, uses relative symlinks for `.agents/` and `.claude/`
136	
137	## Requirements
138	
139	### Functional
140	
141	#### install.sh (curl script)
142	
143	1. **Binary-Only Installation**
144	   - Downloads `ddx` binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx`
145	   - Sets up PATH in shell rc file
146	   - Sets up shell completions
147	   - Does NOT create `~/.ddx/`, `~/.agents/`, or `~/.claude/`
148	
149	#### Global Installation (`ddx install --global`)
150	
151	2. **DDx Skills Extraction**
152	   - Extracts embedded skills (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review, ddx-run) to `~/.ddx/skills/`
153	   - Creates `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*` symlinks в†’ `~/.ddx/skills/ddx-*`
154	   - Creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` symlinks в†’ `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`
155	
156	#### Repository Initialization (`ddx init`)
157	
158	3. **Project Structure Creation**
159	   - Creates `.ddx/` directory with config.yaml and library structure
160	   - Copies bootstrap skills (ddx-doctor, ddx-run) as **real files** to `.ddx/skills/`
161	   - Copies bootstrap skills to `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` as **real files**
162	   - All files are git-trackable (no symlinks for project-local skills)
163	
164	3a. **Bootstrap Skill Cleanup (Stale ddx-* Removal)**
165	   - Before copying bootstrap skills, scans each target directory (`.ddx/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`) for existing `ddx-*` subdirectories
166	   - Any `ddx-*` directory containing a `SKILL.md` that is **not** in the current bootstrap allowlist (`ddx-doctor`, `ddx-run`) is removed
167	   - Purpose: removes skills from older DDx versions that are no longer part of the bootstrap set
168	   - Only removes `ddx-*` prefixed directories; plugin skills (e.g., `helix-*`) are never touched
169	   - Silent: no user-visible output on cleanup; errors are ignored (non-fatal)
170	   - Runs on every `ddx init` invocation, including `ddx init --force`
171	
172	4. **Pre-flight Check**
173	   - Verify `ddx` binary exists in PATH
174	   - If missing: warn user, suggest running install.sh
175	
176	#### Plugin Installation (`ddx install <plugin>`)
177	
178	5. **Project-Scoped Plugin Install**
179	   - Default: downloads released tarball from plugin's GitHub releases
180	   - Extracts to `$PROJECT/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
181	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.agents/skills/<skill>` в†’ `.ddx/plugins/<name>/.agents/skills/<skill>`
182	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.claude/skills/<skill>` в†’ `.agents/skills/<skill>`
183	   - Relative symlinks work across clone/checkout (no absolute paths)
184	   - Fallback: `ddx install <plugin> --from-source` clones repo (for developers working on the plugin)
185	
186	5a. **Plugin Skill Stale Link Pruning**
187	   - Before creating new skill symlinks, scans the target skills directory for existing entries not in the plugin's current skill list
188	   - Removes a symlink only if **both** conditions hold: (a) it is a symlink (not a real file), and (b) its resolved target is within the plugin's installed root (`.ddx/plugins/<name>/`)
189	   - Purpose: removes symlinks for skills that were removed in a plugin update
190	   - Real files (e.g., bootstrap skills copied by `ddx init`) are never removed by this step
191	   - Symlinks from other plugins (pointing outside this plugin's root) are never removed
192	   - Runs during every `ddx install <plugin>` invocation
193	
194	5b. **Stale Install File Removal**
195	   - The plugin registry tracks the set of files written by each install
196	   - On re-install (update), files from the previous install that are absent from the new install's file list are removed
197	   - Applies to any file tracked in the plugin registry entry (e.g., extracted tarball contents)
198	   - Runs during `ddx install <plugin>` when a prior install record exists
199	
200	6. **Plugin Manifest**
201	   - Records installed plugins in `.ddx/plugins.yaml` or similar
202	   - Tracks name, version, install source (release vs source), install date
203	   - Enables `ddx installed` to show project-scoped plugins
204	   - Enables `ddx outdated` to check for newer released versions
205	
206	#### Version Tracking & Staleness Detection
207	
208	9. **Project Version Stamp (`.ddx/versions.yaml`)**
209	   - System-managed file, separate from user config. Users never edit.
210	   - Written by `ddx init`, committed to git alongside config.yaml
211	   - Contains single field: `ddx_version` вЂ” the binary version that initialized/last updated the project
212	   - Schema:
213	     ```yaml
214	     ddx_version: "0.3.0"
215	     ```
216	
217	10. **Version Gate (pre-run, every command)**
218	    - If `.ddx/versions.yaml` does not exist в†’ skip (not a DDx project, or pre-versioning)
219	    - If binary version is `"dev"` в†’ skip (development builds bypass gate)
220	    - If binary version < project's `ddx_version` в†’ **hard error, block execution:**
221	      ```
222	      Error: This project requires DDx v0.4.0 or newer (you have v0.3.0).
223	      Run 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary.
224	      ```
225	    - Exempt commands: `upgrade`, `version`, `doctor`, `init` (must work even when binary is too old)
226	    - Runs in `PersistentPreRunE`, after config init, before update check
227	    - Pure string compare вЂ” no network, no disk beyond the config read
228	
229	11. **Staleness Hints (post-run, every command)**
230	    - If binary version > project's `ddx_version` в†’ soft hint:
231	      ```
232	      рџ’Ў Project skills from DDx v0.3.0 (you have v0.4.0). Run 'ddx init --force' to update.
233	      ```
234	    - Plugin staleness: compare `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries vs `BuiltinRegistry()` в†’ soft hint:
235	      ```
236	      рџ’Ў helix 1.0.0 installed, 2.0.0 available. Run 'ddx install helix' to update.
237	      ```
238	    - Runs in `PersistentPostRunE`, after existing update-available banner
239	    - Pure local comparisons вЂ” no network
240	
241	12. **Force Refresh (`ddx init --force`)**
242	    - Overwrites `.ddx/versions.yaml` with current binary version
243	    - Overwrites existing skill files (currently `registerProjectSkills` skips existing files вЂ” must add `force` parameter)
244	    - Preserves user config in `.ddx/config.yaml`
245	
246	#### Updates
247	
248	7. **Binary Update (`ddx upgrade`)**
249	   - Checks GitHub for new ddx release (async, 24h cache via `~/.cache/ddx/last-update-check.json`)
250	   - Uses GitHub `releases/latest`, so prereleases do not trigger normal update detection unless intentionally promoted as the latest release
251	   - Version comparison must treat any hyphenated prerelease suffix as older than the matching stable release, with channel ordering `alpha < beta < rc < stable` and numeric ordering within the same channel (`rc2 < rc10`)
252	   - Downloads and replaces binary
253	   - After upgrade, next command in project shows staleness hint (correct: new binary > old `ddx_version`)
254	   - Dogfood installs of prereleases remain possible via explicit version selection, e.g. `DDX_VERSION=v0.3.0-rc1 curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash`
255	
256	8. **Plugin Update (`ddx update <plugin>`)**
257	   - Checks plugin's GitHub releases for newer version
258	   - Downloads new release tarball to `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
259	   - Re-establishes relative symlinks
260	   - `ddx update <plugin> --from-source` re-clones from repo HEAD
261	
262	### Non-Functional
263	
264	- **No Repo Bloat:** plugins live in `.ddx/plugins/` (gitignored or committed per user preference)
265	- **Git-Trackable Skills:** `ddx init` copies real files, not symlinks
266	- **Git-Trackable Versions:** `.ddx/versions.yaml` committed to git вЂ” teammates get version gate on clone
267	- **Git-Trackable Execution Evidence:** `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` is
268	  the tracked execute-bead attempt bundle defined in FEAT-006 В§"Execute-Bead
269	  Evidence Bundle". `ddx init` and any DDx-managed `.gitignore` template
270	  must leave `.ddx/executions/` trackable so bundles committed by
271	  `execute-bead` survive clones. Only the ignored runtime scratch paths
272	  listed in FEAT-006 (`.ddx/exec-runs.d/`, `.ddx/agent-logs/`,
273	  `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*/`) may be excluded from tracking.
274	- **Relative Symlinks for Plugins:** work across machines, no absolute paths
275	- **No Windows Targets:** relative symlinks are acceptable
276	- **Offline-First:** bootstrap skills work without network; version gate is local-only
277	- **Idempotent:** multiple runs of same command produce same result
278	- **Separation of Concerns:** `.ddx/config.yaml` for user preferences, `.ddx/versions.yaml` for system-managed state, `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` for global plugin state
279	
280	## Architecture
281	
282	### Directory Structure
283	
284	```
285	# Global (via ddx install --global)
286	~/.ddx/
287	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ skills/
288	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-bead/
289	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-agent/
290	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-install/
291	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-status/
292	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-review/
293	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ddx-run/
294	в””в”Ђв”Ђ config.yaml
295	
296	~/.agents/skills/
297	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-bead/ в†’ ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-bead/
298	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-agent/ в†’ ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-agent/
299	в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
300	
301	~/.claude/skills/
302	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-bead/ в†’ ~/.agents/skills/ddx-bead/
303	в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
304	
305	# Project (via ddx init + ddx install helix)
306	project/
307	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .ddx/
308	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config.yaml       (user preferences)
309	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ versions.yaml     (system-managed: ddx_version)
310	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ library/
311	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ skills/
312	в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-doctor/   (real files, git-tracked)
313	в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ddx-run/      (real files, git-tracked)
314	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ executions/       (tracked execute-bead attempt bundles; see FEAT-006)
315	в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ <attempt-id>/ (prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, ...)
316	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ plugins/
317	в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ helix/        (cloned plugin)
318	в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ .agents/skills/
319	в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helix-align/
320	в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helix-build/
321	в”‚               в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
322	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .agents/skills/
323	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-doctor/       (real files, copied by ddx init)
324	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-run/          (real files, copied by ddx init)
325	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helix-align/ в†’ ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
326	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helix-build/ в†’ ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-build
327	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
328	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/skills/
329	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ddx-doctor/ в†’ ../.agents/skills/ddx-doctor
330	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helix-align/ в†’ ../.agents/skills/helix-align
331	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
332	в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
333	```
334	
335	### Command Matrix
336	
337	| Command | Scope | What It Does |
338	|---------|-------|--------------|
339	| `curl install.sh \| bash` | Global | Binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx` + PATH |
340	| `ddx install --global` | Global | Extract skills to `~/.ddx/`, symlink `~/.agents/`, `~/.claude/` |
341	| `ddx init` | Project | `.ddx/` structure + copy bootstrap skills to `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
342	| `ddx install helix` | Project | Clone to `.ddx/plugins/helix/`, relative symlinks in `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
343	| `ddx upgrade` | Global | Update binary |
344	| `ddx update <plugin>` | Project | Re-clone plugin, re-establish symlinks |
345	
346	### Key Design Decisions
347	
348	1. **Copy over symlink for ddx init**: Git doesn't track symlinks well. Bootstrap skills must survive `git clone` on a fresh machine.
349	
350	2. **Relative symlinks for plugins**: Plugin skills are installed via relative symlinks (e.g., `../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align`). This works across machines without absolute paths. Acceptable since we're not targeting Windows.
351	
352	3. **Project-scoped plugins**: Plugins install to the project, not globally. This lets different projects use different plugin versions and makes the project self-contained.
353	
354	4. **Minimal install.sh**: The curl script does one thing (install binary). Everything else is handled by `ddx` commands that have proper error handling, embedded assets, and testability.
355	
356	## Out of Scope
357	
358	- Windows-specific installation (future)
359	- System package manager integration (apt, brew, etc.) (future)
360	- Plugin publishing to registry (future)
361	- Global plugin installation (future вЂ” currently project-scoped only)
362	
363	## Acceptance Criteria
364	
365	### AC-001: Clean Machine Installation
366	```bash
367	# In Docker container with nothing installed
368	curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash
369	which ddx            # в†’ ~/.local/bin/ddx
370	ddx version          # в†’ shows version
371	ls ~/.ddx/ 2>&1      # в†’ no such directory (install.sh doesn't create it)
372	```
373	
374	### AC-002: Global Skill Installation
375	Post-consolidation (FEAT-011, 2026-04-17): the embedded tree is the
376	single portable `ddx` skill. Adjust the example from the pre-consolidation
377	`ddx-bead`/`ddx-agent`/etc. listing to match the current layout:
378	
379	```bash
380	# After AC-001
381	ddx install --global
382	ls ~/.ddx/skills/ddx/           # в†’ skill files exist (SKILL.md, reference/*.md)
383	readlink ~/.agents/skills/ddx   # в†’ ../../.ddx/skills/ddx
384	readlink ~/.claude/skills/ddx   # в†’ ../../.agents/skills/ddx
385	```
386	
387	Implementation: `cli/cmd/install_global.go`. Covered by
388	`TestInstallGlobalExtractsSkillsAndChainsSymlinks` and the adjacent
389	idempotency / safety tests.
390	
391	### AC-003: Repository Initialization
392	```bash
393	# In empty project directory
394	ddx init
395	ls .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/   # в†’ real files (not symlinks)
396	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/ # в†’ real files (copied, not symlinked)
397	ls .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/ # в†’ real files or relative symlink to .agents
398	git add .agents/ .claude/     # в†’ works (real files tracked by git)
399	```
400	
401	### AC-004: Plugin Installation (Project-Scoped)
402	```bash
403	# In initialized project
404	ddx install helix
405	ls .ddx/plugins/helix/                        # в†’ plugin cloned
406	readlink .agents/skills/helix-align           # в†’ ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
407	readlink .claude/skills/helix-align           # в†’ ../.agents/skills/helix-align
408	```
409	
410	### AC-005: Missing DDx Detection
411	```bash
412	# Clone a project with .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/ but no ddx binary
413	# ddx-doctor skill detects missing binary and prompts install
414	```
415	
416	### AC-006: Version Tracking
417	```bash
418	# ddx init writes versions.yaml
419	ddx init
420	cat .ddx/versions.yaml  # в†’ ddx_version: "0.3.0" (current binary version)
421	git log --oneline -1     # в†’ commit includes .ddx/versions.yaml
422	```
423	
424	### AC-007: Version Gate (binary too old)
425	```bash
426	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to require newer version
427	echo 'ddx_version: "99.0.0"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
428	ddx bead list            # в†’ Error: This project requires DDx v99.0.0 or newer...
429	ddx version              # в†’ works (exempt command)
430	ddx upgrade              # в†’ works (exempt command)
431	```
432	
433	### AC-008: Staleness Hint (binary newer)
434	```bash
435	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to older version
436	echo 'ddx_version: "0.0.1"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
437	ddx bead list            # в†’ normal output + hint: "рџ’Ў Project skills from DDx v0.0.1..."
438	```
439	
440	### AC-009: Force Refresh
441	```bash
442	# After staleness hint
443	ddx init --force
444	cat .ddx/versions.yaml   # в†’ ddx_version updated to current
445	cat .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/SKILL.md  # в†’ overwritten with latest
446	```
447	
448	### AC-010: Dev Build Bypass
449	```bash
450	# Dev build (version="dev") should not trigger gate
451	# Even if versions.yaml says v99.0.0
452	ddx bead list            # в†’ works normally, no error
453	```
454	
455	### AC-011: Docker Test Coverage
456	All above scenarios run in Docker containers:
457	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.clean` вЂ” minimal image for AC-001
458	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.with-ddx` вЂ” pre-installed for AC-002, AC-003, AC-004
459	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.no-binary` вЂ” ddx removed for AC-005
460	
461	### AC-012: Bootstrap Skill Cleanup on `ddx init`
462	```bash
463	# Simulate a stale bootstrap skill from an older DDx version
464	mkdir -p .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill
465	echo "---" > .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill/SKILL.md
466	ddx init
467	ls .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill 2>&1  # в†’ no such file or directory (removed)
468	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/         # в†’ present (in bootstrap allowlist)
469	ls .agents/skills/ddx-run/            # в†’ present (in bootstrap allowlist)
470	# Plugin skills are untouched
471	ls .agents/skills/helix-align 2>&1    # в†’ unchanged (not a ddx-* prefix)
472	```
473	
474	### AC-013: Plugin Skill Stale Link Pruning on `ddx install`
475	```bash
476	# Install plugin, then re-install after a skill is removed upstream
477	ddx install helix                         # installs helix-align, helix-build
478	# Simulate helix-build removed in new version (re-install with fewer skills)
479	ddx install helix
480	ls .agents/skills/helix-build 2>&1        # в†’ no such file or directory (stale link removed)
481	ls .agents/skills/helix-align             # в†’ symlink present (still in plugin)
482	# Bootstrap skills are not removed
483	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/             # в†’ unchanged (real file, not symlink)
484	```
485	
486	### AC-014: Stale Install File Removal on Plugin Update
487	```bash
488	# Install plugin, verify old files removed on update
489	ddx install helix@1.0.0
490	# Files from 1.0.0 that don't exist in 1.1.0 are removed when updating
491	ddx install helix@1.1.0
492	# Files only in 1.0.0 are gone; files in 1.1.0 are present

9	---
10	# Feature: DDx Agent Skills
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-011
13	**Status:** Revising (consolidation in progress; see Phase 1 epic in beads)
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	DDx ships a single agent-facing skill (`ddx`) that makes any
20	skills-compatible coding agent "ddx-aware" after `ddx init`. The skill
21	is written to the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) open
22	standard so it works identically in Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
23	CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, and any other harness that implements the
24	standard.
25	
26	When the user says "do work", "review this", "what's on the queue",
27	"create a bead", or any DDx concept, the harness discovers the `ddx`
28	skill via its description, loads `SKILL.md`, and routes via an
29	explicit intent table into `reference/*.md` files that contain the
30	domain guidance.
31	
32	## Problem Statement
33	
34	Prior iterations of FEAT-011 shipped ~7 sibling skills
35	(`ddx-bead`, `ddx-agent`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
36	`ddx-install`, `ddx-doctor`). Real-world usage exposed problems:
37	
38	- **Intent ambiguity.** Users say "do work", not "/ddx-run". A flat
39	  list of named slash commands forces the harness to guess between
40	  `/ddx-bead` vs `/ddx-run` vs `/ddx-work` from natural-language
41	  phrases.
42	- **Vocabulary drift.** Each skill redefined terms (bead, queue,
43	  harness, review) inline; wording diverged across files and from
44	  FEAT-* specs.
45	- **Workflow-opinion leakage.** `ddx-bead` mandated `helix` labels
46	  and documented `phase:*` labels вЂ” HELIX methodology opinions
47	  inside a core DDx skill.
48	- **Skill tree drift.** Two copies of most skills
49	  (`cli/internal/skills/` embedded vs top-level `/skills/`) with
50	  real content divergence.
51	- **Init gap.** `ddx init` copied only 2 of 7 embedded skills; the
52	  rest never surfaced to Claude Code unless the user re-installed
53	  manually.
54	- **Portability.** Skills used Claude-Code-only frontmatter fields
55	  (`argument-hint`) that are silently ignored by Codex and other
56	  harnesses, and reached for Claude-Code-only patterns
57	  (`context: fork`) that don't exist elsewhere.
58	
59	The consolidated design fixes each of these.
60	
61	## Architecture
62	
63	### Single skill, progressive disclosure
64	
65	```
66	skills/ddx/
67	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md                # overview, vocabulary, intent-router directive
68	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ reference/
69	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ beads.md            # writing execution-ready beads (best practices)
70	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ work.md             # draining the queue, execute-bead, verify + close
71	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ review.md           # bead-review (AC grade) + quorum code review
72	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agents.md           # harness/profile dispatch, personas
73	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ status.md           # queue state, doctor, health checks
74	в””в”Ђв”Ђ evals/
75	    в””в”Ђв”Ђ routing.jsonl       # ~15 phraseв†’expected-routing fixtures
76	```
77	
78	At harness startup, only the skill's `name` + `description` metadata
79	is pre-loaded. On activation, the harness reads `SKILL.md`. When the
80	intent router matches a phrase, the harness reads the matching
81	`reference/*.md`. Nothing else loads. This is the Anthropic
82	"Pattern 2: Domain-specific organization" pattern and matches how
83	Codex and other agentskills.io implementations progressively disclose
84	skill content.
85	
86	### Portability contract
87	
88	Frontmatter is the portable-safe minimum вЂ” `name` + `description` only.
89	
90	```yaml
91	---
92	name: ddx
93	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
94	---
95	```
96	
97	No `argument-hint`, `when_to_use`, `context: fork`, `allowed-tools`,
98	`disable-model-invocation`, `user-invocable`, `paths`, `model`,
99	`effort`, `agent`, or `hooks` fields вЂ” those are Claude-Code
100	extensions that Codex and others ignore or reject. The description
101	front-loads the DDx nouns (bead, queue, execution, harness, persona,
102	spec-id) **and** the exact verb phrases users say verbatim, because
103	implicit-invocation matchers prefer substring-ish keyword matching
104	over semantic understanding.
105	
106	### Intent-router directive
107	
108	Claude does not reliably auto-chase reference links; the router in
109	`SKILL.md` is stated as an **explicit directive**, not a hint:
110	
111	> "Before responding to any DDx-related request, read the matching
112	> reference file below. The router is not optional вЂ” your answer must
113	> be grounded in the reference file's guidance, not this overview
114	> alone."
115	
116	### Subagents: ship none
117	
118	Subagent orchestration is harness-specific (Claude Code has
119	`.claude/agents/` + `context: fork`; Codex has its own subagent
120	surface; others differ). The `ddx` skill describes *actions*
121	("run `ddx agent run --quorum=<policy>`") and lets each harness
122	decide how to run them. Quorum review is a CLI invocation, not a
123	skill-frontmatter directive.
124	
125	### Installation
126	
127	> See FEAT-015 (2026-04-29 amendment) for the authoritative install
128	> model: project-local copy semantics, zero symlinks created by
129	> `ddx install`, and the no-`~` manifest invariant.
130	
131	- `ddx init` copies `skills/ddx/` into `.claude/skills/ddx/`,
132	  `.agents/skills/ddx/`, and `.ddx/skills/ddx/` as real files
133	  (symlinks break after `git clone` on a fresh machine, and DDx no
134	  longer creates symlinks during install вЂ” see FEAT-015).
135	- On init and on `ddx update`, stale ddx-prefixed skill directories
136	  from prior DDx versions are removed:
137	  `ddx-bead`, `ddx-run`, `ddx-agent`, `ddx-review`, `ddx-status`,
138	  `ddx-doctor`, `ddx-install`, `ddx-release`. Third-party skills are
139	  untouched.
140	- Skills embed into the binary via `//go:embed all:ddx` against a
141	  copy under `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`. Because `go:embed` cannot
142	  traverse upward, a `make copy-skills` target rsyncs
143	  `skills/ddx/` в†’ `cli/internal/skills/ddx/` before every build.
144	
145	### AGENTS.md: merge, not clobber
146	
147	Codex treats `AGENTS.md` as primary guidance before work, and users
148	may have added content. `ddx init` uses marker-delimited injection:
149	
150	```markdown
151	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->
152	This project uses DDx. Use the `ddx` skill for beads, work, review,
153	agents, and status.
154	
155	(tracker/merge policy follows)
156	<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->
157	```
158	
159	Content outside the markers is preserved. The block says
160	"the `ddx` skill", not "`/ddx`" (which is Claude-specific slash-
161	command phrasing). Re-running `ddx init` updates the block in place.
162	
163	### Evaluation-driven validation
164	
165	Anthropic's skill-authoring guidance treats evaluations as
166	load-bearing, not optional. The repo ships:
167	
168	- `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` вЂ” ~15 rows, each a user phrase +
169	  expected reference file + expected CLI invocation, covering every
170	  intent-router entry and edge phrasings.
171	- `scripts/eval-skill.sh` вЂ” driver that runs each row against
172	  `--harness claude` and `--harness codex` and verifies routing.
173	  `--validate` mode does agentskills.io spec conformance.
174	- `make eval-skill` in CI on PRs that touch `skills/ddx/`.
175	
176	## Requirements
177	
178	### Functional
179	
180	1. DDx ships exactly one skill (`ddx`) in `skills/ddx/`; no sibling
181	   `ddx-*` skill directories.
182	2. `SKILL.md` frontmatter contains only `name` and `description`.
183	3. `SKILL.md` body is under 500 lines and includes an explicit
184	   intent-router directive.
185	4. `reference/*.md` files are linked one level deep from `SKILL.md`.
186	5. `ddx init` copies the skill, removes stale `ddx-*` dirs, and
187	   merges the AGENTS.md block without clobbering user content.
188	6. `ddx update` refreshes `.claude/skills/ddx/` and removes stale
189	   dirs.
190	7. `skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl` contains at least 15 rows, each
191	   passing against `claude` and `codex` harnesses via
192	   `make eval-skill`.
193	8. `SKILL.md` passes `scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate` (agentskills.io
194	   spec conformance).
195	
196	### Non-Functional
197	
198	- Skills work with any agent supporting the agentskills.io standard;
199	  no Claude-Code-only frontmatter or directives.
200	- Skills are plain Markdown вЂ” no runtime dependencies.
201	- Skills degrade gracefully if DDx CLI is not installed (clear error).
202	- HELIX-specific rules (`helix` label requirement, `phase:*`
203	  enumeration) do not appear in the `ddx` skill; HELIX opinions ship
204	  in the HELIX plugin.
205	
206	## User Stories
207	
208	### US-110: Harness routes natural-language DDx intent
209	**As a** user in a DDx project using Claude Code, Codex, or any other
210	skills-compatible harness
211	**I want** phrases like "do work", "drain the queue", "review this",
212	"what's on the queue", "create a bead" to route to DDx guidance
213	**So that** I don't have to remember slash-command names
214	
215	**Acceptance Criteria:**
216	- Running `make eval-skill` passes all rows against both `--harness claude`
217	  and `--harness codex`.
218	- Each intent-router entry in `SKILL.md` has at least one matching
219	  row in `routing.jsonl`.
220	
221	### US-111: Skill stays under the token budget
222	**As a** harness loading the `ddx` skill
223	**I want** `SKILL.md` body under 500 lines
224	**So that** skill activation stays within the Anthropic-recommended
225	token budget and doesn't compete with conversation context
226	
227	**Acceptance Criteria:**
228	- `wc -l skills/ddx/SKILL.md` < 500.
229	- `scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate` passes.
230	
231	### US-112: `ddx init` handles existing projects cleanly
232	**As a** user upgrading from an older DDx version with
233	`.claude/skills/ddx-run/` and similar dirs already present
234	**I want** `ddx init` to remove the old dirs and install only the
235	new single-skill layout
236	**So that** the harness doesn't see stale, conflicting skills
237	
238	**Acceptance Criteria:**
239	- In a dir pre-seeded with
240	  `.claude/skills/{ddx-run,ddx-doctor,ddx-bead}/`, running
241	  `ddx init` leaves only `.claude/skills/ddx/`.
242	- Third-party skills under `.claude/skills/` are untouched.
243	
244	### US-113: `AGENTS.md` merge preserves user content
245	**As a** user who has added content to `AGENTS.md`
246	**I want** `ddx init` to inject the DDx block without clobbering
247	what I wrote
248	**So that** my Codex / Claude / Gemini setup isn't broken
249	
250	**Acceptance Criteria:**
251	- Given an `AGENTS.md` with user content both before and after the
252	  `<!-- DDX-AGENTS:START -->` / `<!-- DDX-AGENTS:END -->` markers,
253	  running `ddx init` updates the block between markers and preserves
254	  everything outside.
255	- Running `ddx init` a second time does not duplicate the block.
256	
257	### US-114: `ddx update` refreshes skills
258	**As a** user who ran `ddx init` on an older DDx version
259	**I want** `ddx update` to refresh `.claude/skills/ddx/` to the
260	current shipped content
261	**So that** I don't have to re-run `ddx init` to pick up skill
262	improvements
263	
264	**Acceptance Criteria:**
265	- After `ddx update`, `.claude/skills/ddx/` bytes match the embedded
266	  skill content.
267	- Stale `ddx-*` dirs are removed as in US-112.
268	
269	## Dependencies
270	
271	- FEAT-001 (CLI commands the skill wraps)
272	- FEAT-006 (agent service вЂ” harnesses, profiles, personas)
273	- FEAT-009 (registry for package-install guidance inside `reference/agents.md`)
274	- agentskills.io open standard
275	
276	## Out of Scope
277	
278	- Workflow-specific skills (HELIX provides `helix-*` in its own plugin).
279	- Claude-Code-specific skill features (`context: fork`, `allowed-tools`,
280	  `paths`, subagents under `.claude/agents/`) вЂ” portability > optimization.
281	- A CI-enforced vocabulary drift guard. One skill + one glossary is
282	  self-policing. Revisit only if drift recurs.
283	
284	## Implementation Notes
285	
286	The migration from the 7-sibling-skills layout to the single `ddx`
287	skill is sequenced into phases; see the Phase 1 / Phase 2 / Phase 3
288	epic beads for the work breakdown. Phase 1 is the critical path
289	(ship the new surface + eval suite + init/update changes); Phase 2
290	is cleanup of old references; Phase 3 is the persona roster trim
291	(FEAT-006 scope, not blocking this feature).
292

2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-009
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	---
7	# Feature: Online Library & Plugin Registry
8	
9	> **FEAT-015 amendment (2026-04-29):** Install paths described in this document
10	> (global home-directory targets) describe the pre-amendment model.
11	> The authoritative install architecture is in FEAT-015. All installs are now
12	> project-local under `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `.agents/skills/`, and
13	> `.claude/skills/`. No home-directory writes occur during `ddx install`.
14	
15	**ID:** FEAT-009
16	**Status:** Complete
17	**Priority:** P0
18	**Owner:** DDx Team
19	
20	## Overview
21	
22	DDx needs to discover, install, and manage resources from an online library. The library is a git repository (`ddx-library`) containing personas, prompts, templates, patterns, MCP configurations, and **package descriptors** for workflow tools like HELIX. DDx fetches resources on demand вЂ” no git subtree, no full repo clone.
23	
24	This replaces the current git-subtree-based sync model with a lighter, more practical approach: DDx downloads what you ask for, caches it locally, and keeps track of what's installed.
25	
26	## Problem Statement
27	
28	**Current situation:**
29	- `ddx-library` exists as a git repo but DDx's sync mechanism (`ddx update`) is a stub
30	- Git subtree is complex, fragile, and overkill for distributing personas and templates
31	- HELIX publishes skills independently (global home paths) with no DDx integration
32	- Dun expects plugins at `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` but nothing populates this path
33	- There's no way to discover what's available or install a specific resource
34	
35	**Desired outcome:** `ddx install helix` fetches and installs HELIX skills. `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer` fetches one persona. `ddx search testing` finds testing-related resources. Simple, practical, no git subtree.
36	
37	## Architecture
38	
39	### Registries
40	
41	DDx supports multiple registries. Each registry is a git repository containing a `registry.yaml` index and installable resources. Registries are checked in order вЂ” first match wins.
42	
43	```yaml
44	# .ddx/config.yaml
45	registries:
46	  - url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library     # default, always present
47	    branch: main
48	  - url: https://github.com/mycompany/ddx-private  # company-private
49	    branch: main
50	```
51	
52	The default registry (`https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library`) is always included even if not explicitly listed. Additional registries are additive вЂ” they extend the default, not replace it.
53	
54	### Registry Repository Structure
55	
56	Each registry repo has:
57	
58	```
59	ddx-library/
60	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ registry.yaml              # Index of all available packages
61	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ personas/
62	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ strict-code-reviewer.md
63	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pragmatic-implementer.md
64	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
65	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prompts/
66	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
67	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/
68	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
69	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ patterns/
70	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
71	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ artifacts/                 # Artifact type resources
72	в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ adr/
73	в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ template.md
74	в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ create.md
75	в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ evolve.md
76	в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ check.md
77	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
78	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mcp-servers/
79	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ registry.yml
80	в”њв”Ђв”Ђ workflows/
81	в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ helix/
82	в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ package.yaml       # HELIX package descriptor
83	в””в”Ђв”Ђ plugins/
84	    в””в”Ђв”Ђ helix/
85	        в””в”Ђв”Ђ plugin.yaml        # Dun plugin for HELIX checks
86	```
87	
88	### Package Descriptor
89	
90	Workflow tools and plugins publish a `package.yaml` in the library:
91	
92	```yaml
93	name: helix
94	version: 1.0.0
95	description: Structured development workflow with AI-assisted collaboration
96	type: workflow                  # workflow | plugin | persona-pack | template-pack
97	source: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/helix
98	install:
99	  skills:
100	    source: .agents/skills/     # Path in source repo
101	    target: .agents/skills/     # Install destination (project-local; FEAT-015)
102	  scripts:
103	    source: scripts/helix
104	    target: ~/.local/bin/helix
105	requires:
106	  - ddx >= 0.2.0
107	```
108	
109	### Install Flow
110	
111	> **Amended by FEAT-015 (2026-04-29):** `ddx install <plugin>` is now
112	> project-local. Skills land in `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
113	> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/` as real files (no symlinks, no home-
114	> directory writes). The global home-directory install targets and
115	> `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` flow described below are historical context
116	> from the pre-amendment model. See FEAT-015 for the authoritative
117	> install semantics and the no-tilde-prefix manifest invariant.
118	
119	```bash
120	ddx install helix
121	```
122	
123	1. Fetch `registry.yaml` from ddx-library (cached, refreshed on `ddx update`)
124	2. Find the `helix` entry в†’ read `package.yaml`
125	3. Clone/download the source repo (shallow, to temp dir)
126	4. Copy skills to `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/helix-*` (FEAT-015: project-local)
127	5. Copy scripts to `~/.local/bin/helix`
128	6. Record installation in `.ddx/installed.yaml`
129	
130	For simple resources (individual personas, templates):
131	
132	```bash
133	ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer
134	```
135	
136	1. Fetch the file directly from ddx-library (via GitHub raw URL or git archive)
137	2. Copy to `.ddx/library/personas/strict-code-reviewer.md`
138	
139	### Cache and State
140	
141	- **Registry cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/registries/<name>/registry.yaml` (one per registry, refreshed by `ddx update`)
142	- **Installation record:** `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` (what's installed, versions, timestamps, source registry)
143	- **Library cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/library/` (downloaded resources)
144	- **Plugin cache:** `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` (populated for dun discovery)
145	
146	## Requirements
147	
148	### Functional
149	
150	1. **Registry fetch** (`ddx update`) вЂ” download latest `registry.yaml` from ddx-library
151	2. **Search** (`ddx search <query>`) вЂ” search available resources by name, type, or keyword
152	3. **Install resource** (`ddx install <name>`) вЂ” download and install a specific resource or package
153	4. **Install workflow** (`ddx install helix`) вЂ” full workflow installation (skills, scripts, plugins)
154	5. **List installed** (`ddx installed`) вЂ” show what's installed locally
155	6. **Uninstall** (`ddx uninstall <name>`) вЂ” remove an installed resource
156	7. **Populate plugin cache** вЂ” on install, copy dun-compatible plugins to `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/`
157	8. **Version tracking** вЂ” record installed versions, detect available updates
158	9. **Update detection** (`ddx outdated`) вЂ” compare installed package versions
159	   against source repo tags (via `git ls-remote --tags`) to determine if
160	   updates are available. Output: package name, installed version, latest
161	   available, update available (yes/no).
162	10. **Package update** (`ddx upgrade <name>`) вЂ” re-install a package at the
163	    latest available version. Performs a fresh shallow clone at the latest tag,
164	    copies new files, updates `installed.yaml`. Safe to run repeatedly.
165	11. **Startup update check** вЂ” on `ddx` startup (async, non-blocking), check
166	    if installed packages have newer versions available. If so, print a
167	    one-line notice: `Plugin update available: helix 0.1.0 в†’ 0.2.0 (run
168	    'ddx upgrade helix')`. Same pattern as the existing DDx binary update
169	    check.
170	
171	### Non-Functional
172	
173	- **No git subtree** вЂ” fetch individual files or shallow clones, not full repo history
174	- **Offline-safe** вЂ” work from cache when offline; warn but don't fail
175	- **Idempotent** вЂ” running `ddx install helix` twice is safe
176	- **Fast** вЂ” individual resource install <5s on broadband
177	
178	## CLI Commands
179	
180	```bash
181	ddx update                          # Refresh registry from upstream
182	ddx search <query>                  # Search available resources
183	ddx install <name>                  # Install a resource or package
184	ddx install helix                   # Install HELIX workflow
185	ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer  # Install one persona
186	ddx installed                       # List what's installed
187	ddx outdated                        # Check for available updates
188	ddx upgrade <name>                  # Update a package to latest version
189	ddx uninstall <name>                # Remove an installed resource
190	```
191	
192	## User Stories
193	
194	### US-090: Developer Discovers Available Workflows
195	**As a** developer evaluating DDx
196	**I want** to search for available workflow tools
197	**So that** I can find and install HELIX or other methodologies
198	
199	**Acceptance Criteria:**
200	- Given I run `ddx search workflow`, then I see HELIX and any other registered workflows with descriptions
201	- Given I run `ddx install helix`, then HELIX skills are installed to `.agents/skills/` (project-local; FEAT-015) and the CLI to `~/.local/bin/`
202	
203	### US-091: Developer Installs Individual Resources
204	**As a** developer customizing my project
205	**I want** to install specific personas or templates from the library
206	**So that** I get exactly what I need without bulk downloading
207	
208	**Acceptance Criteria:**
209	- Given I run `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer`, then the persona file is copied to `.ddx/library/personas/`
210	- Given I run `ddx installed`, then I see `persona/strict-code-reviewer` with version and install date
211	
212	## Migration from Git Subtree
213	
214	The current `ddx init` creates a git subtree for `.ddx/library/`. This should be replaced:
215	
216	1. **New projects:** `ddx init` creates `.ddx/library/` as a local directory (no subtree)
217	2. **Library population:** `ddx install` fetches resources on demand
218	3. **Existing projects:** The git subtree continues to work but is no longer the recommended flow
219	4. **`ddx contribute`:** Remains as a way to push changes back (creates a PR against ddx-library)
220	
221	## Dependencies
222	
223	- `ddx-library` repo with `registry.yaml`
224	- GitHub API or git archive for fetching individual files
225	- Package descriptors in workflow tool repos (HELIX, etc.)
226	
227	## Out of Scope
228	
229	- Package signing or verification (v2)
230	- Automatic updates (manual `ddx update` + `ddx install` for now)
231	- Dependency resolution between packages
232

---
ddx:
  id: FEAT-015
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-001
    - FEAT-009
    - FEAT-011
---
# Feature: DDx Installation Architecture

**ID:** FEAT-015
**Status:** In Progress
**Priority:** P0
**Owner:** DDx Team

> **Update 2026-04-29 (project-local install, no symlinks):** This feature
> is amended to drop the global skill installation model entirely. DDx no
> longer installs skills under `~/.agents/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/`,
> and `ddx install --global` is removed. `ddx init` and `ddx install
> <plugin>` only touch the current `<projectRoot>`. The new project-local
> tree (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`,
> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`) is committed to git as real files.
> **Zero symlinks are created by ddx install** вЂ” see the new
> "Cross-platform invariant" section below for the Windows-compatibility
> reasoning and how plugin-author symlinks are handled. Existing projects
> with home-directory symlinks or stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries
> are handled per the new "Migration" section. Plugin manifests whose
> `Root.Target` begins with `~` are rejected with a non-zero exit.
> Sections, bullets, and acceptance criteria below that describe
> `ddx install --global`, `~/.ddx/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
> or `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` should be read as historical context;
> the amendment in this header and the two new sections are
> authoritative.
>
> **Update 2026-04-17:** The skill roster referenced throughout this
> document (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review,
> ddx-run, ddx-doctor) reflects the pre-consolidation layout. Per
> FEAT-011, DDx now ships a **single portable `ddx` skill** with
> `SKILL.md` + `reference/*.md` (progressive disclosure, agentskills.io
> standard). The installation flow described below is still accurate
> in structure вЂ” binary separate from library, `ddx init` copies skills
> as real files, `ddx install <plugin>` adds plugin-scoped content вЂ”
> but any reference to the 7-skill roster should be read as historical
> context. Sections that specifically describe the bootstrap
> allowlist are now ["ddx"] instead of ["ddx-doctor", "ddx-run"], and
> the stale-skill cleanup removes all old ddx-prefixed dirs (ddx-bead,
> ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install,
> ddx-release) on init and update. See FEAT-011 for the current skill
> architecture.

## Overview

Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
- **install.sh** вЂ” binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
- **ddx init** вЂ” project-local: writes `<projectRoot>/.ddx/`,
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
  as real files (copied, never symlinked). Touches no path outside the
  project root.
- **ddx install \<plugin\>** вЂ” project-local: plugin tarball extracted
  into `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, then plugin skills copied
  as real files into `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Touches no path outside the project
  root. Creates zero symlinks (see "Cross-platform invariant").
- All project-local install outputs (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`,
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`)
  are intended to be committed to git so teammates and CI get the
  same skills on clone without re-running `ddx install`.

## Cross-platform invariant

**`ddx install` (and `ddx init`) creates zero symlinks.** Every file
written is a real file copied from the binary's embedded tree or from
an extracted plugin tarball.

**Reasoning вЂ” Windows compatibility.** Symlinks on Windows require
either Developer Mode, an elevated process, or specific filesystem
features, and behave inconsistently across NTFS, ReFS, and shared
filesystems. A single project-local layout that works identically on
Linux, macOS, and Windows is worth the duplication cost of copied
files.

**Plugin-source symlinks are not materialized.** If a plugin tarball
contains symlinks (e.g. an author symlinks `.claude/skills/foo` в†’
`../.agents/skills/foo` inside their source tree), DDx does **not**
materialize those symlinks on extract. Plugin authors are responsible
for shipping each skill as a real directory in every target the
plugin declares. A plugin that relies on tarball symlinks resolving
on the install side is a malformed plugin.

**Manifest validation.** Plugin manifests are scanned at install
time. Any `Root.Target` (or equivalent install target) whose path
begins with `~` is rejected and `ddx install` exits with a non-zero
status. Targets must be project-relative paths under
`<projectRoot>/`. This invariant is enforced even for plugins that
worked under the pre-amendment global model.

## Migration

Pre-amendment installs may have left state in three places. This
section describes how DDx heals or ignores each.

- **Home-directory symlinked skills** (`~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
  `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` pointing into `~/.ddx/skills/` or a project
  tree). `ddx update --force` (and `ddx init --force` inside an
  existing project) heals the project by rewriting
  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
  as real files. DDx does **not** delete files outside the project
  root; users may `rm -rf ~/.agents/skills/ddx-*
  ~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` themselves if they want a clean home tree.

- **Stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries** from a prior global
  install. These are treated as **no-op uninstalls**: `ddx
  uninstall <name>` succeeds without touching the filesystem when
  the recorded paths are outside the current project root, and the
  entry is removed from `installed.yaml`. No error is raised for
  missing files.

- **`~/.ddx/plugins/`** from a prior global install. DDx leaves this
  directory in place as inert state вЂ” nothing reads from it under
  the project-local model. Users may `rm -rf ~/.ddx/plugins/` at
  their convenience; DDx will not recreate it.

## Problem Statement

Current behavior:
- `install.sh` does too much (creates `~/.ddx/`, sets up symlinks)
- `ddx install helix` clones to user home (`~/.ddx/plugins/`), not project-scoped
- Symlinks aren't tracked by git, so project-local `.agents/skills в†’ .ddx/skills` breaks on clone
- No separation between global installation and project-scoped plugin management
